final_nulls: No public confirmation that Kajaani building 1 (T3-001) is energized/operational as of 2026-07-05. Latest checkpoints: Yle (2026-03-18) still says it 'will be completed in 2026' (future tense); YIT's project page (fetched 2026-07-05) still shows 'Project status: Under construction' with H1 2026 target; DCD (2026-06-18) does not state it is live; fresh web search finds no commissioning/opening announcement. Absence of a commissioning announcement is the finding.

No firmwide GPU figure newer than 'over 25,000' (first published Jan 2025). XTX's website still says 'over 25,000 GPUs' as of 2026-07-05; only the storage figure has been updated (650 PB in Jan 2025 release vs 'over 1 Exabyte' on current site). DCD's June 2026 article likewise still cites 'more than 25,000 GPUs', sourced from job posts.

The ~250MW campus full-build figure appears ONLY in press (DCD Apr 2024; DCD Jan 2025 with 'reportedly'; Yle Apr 2025). Verified absent from XTX's own Kajaani announcement (refetched 2026-07-05: no figure beyond 22.5MW) and from XTX's 96-page 400 kV EIA programme of Apr 2026 (full-text search of the downloaded PDF finds no MW figure at all).
No public annual energy consumption figure (GWh/MWh) exists for the Kajaani campus or firmwide — only IT-power (22.5MW first building) and planned-capacity (~250MW, press-only) figures.

No verifiable public source names Verne Global as the operator hosting XTX's Iceland cluster (T3-002): A-Team Insight (fetched) says only 'a supercomputer in Iceland' and names no operator. No Iceland-specific MW or GPU scale has been published; any Verne Keflavik tenancy attribution should remain 'inferred' at best.

The primary YIT Oyj press release for building 3 (news.cision.com c4363863) returned HTTP 403 again on 2026-07-05, and yitgroup.com's news repository does not mirror it (only the Aug 2025 second-building release is there); DCD's 2026-06-18 write-up therefore remains the best fetchable source and the building-3 fact stays graded 'reported'.
results.reasoning: Adversarial re-verification on 2026-07-05: every URL was fetched (the ymparisto.fi EIA PDF exceeded WebFetch's size cap, so it was downloaded in full — 96 pages, 15MB — and its text extracted and searched directly). All 15 facts survived: every quote was found verbatim in the cited source and values match. Confidence grades were re-checked strictly and stand: XTX's own publication/site = confirmed; YIT contractor/investor pages = confirmed; regulator-hosted EIA filing by the firm = confirmed; Yle/DCD/A-Team = reported. The one date ambiguity (Yle 74-20215962 showing '18.3.' without a year) resolves to 2026-03-18: Yle omits the year only on current-year articles, the article ID postdates the Sept 2025 piece, and its content (second building interior work underway, 2027 completion) matches YIT's investor news of exactly 2026-03-18. Nulls were adversarially tested: fresh searches found no building-1 commissioning announcement (YIT page still 'Under construction' today); XTX's own Kajaani page was re-queried specifically for 250MW and contains none; grep over the full EIA text confirms zero MW figures; the cision primary release for building 3 403'd again and is not mirrored on yitgroup.com, so building 3 correctly stays 'reported' via DCD. No facts dropped, no upgrades or downgrades warranted; nulls all remain true and two were strengthened with fetch-verified detail.

final_nulls: No public facility-total power (MW) figure exists for the existing Dallas/Texas AI training DC (T3-009). Only a per-rack 'up to 140 kW' figure circulates (from the firm's May 2026 video tour); 56 racks x 140 kW ~= 7.8 MW IT load would be arithmetic inference, not public record.

No concrete firmwide GPU fleet number exists beyond 'tens of thousands' (Jane Street's own careers/ML page) and the reported ambition of 'hundreds of thousands' / ~10x compute (Bloomberg via DCD). No exact fleet count is published anywhere.

No GPU count, MW capacity, or contract duration has been disclosed for the $6bn CoreWeave agreement (T3-010). CoreWeave's own press release names only 'NVIDIA's Vera Rubin technology' and delivery 'across multiple facilities', with no facilities identified.

The exact street address and ownership model (owned vs leased colocation) of the Dallas facility are not in the public record. Jane Street's own page says only 'Texas datacenter'; DCD says only 'Dallas, Texas'.

The planned 100-200 MW self-financed facility has no public location, final capacity, or timeline. DCD (citing Bloomberg, verified 2026-07-05): 'the conversations remain in the early stages' and 'Jane Street declined to comment to the publication.'
results.reasoning: Adversarial verification completed 2026-07-05. All four cited URLs were fetched live and every quoted claim was located in the fetched content, so no facts were dropped. Specific checks: (1) janestreet.com/join-jane-street/machine-learning contains 'Today, come tour our new Texas datacenter: 4,032 GPUs, liquid cooled', 'Tens of Thousands of high-end GPUs', and '1 exabytes+ current storage' — confirmed grade upheld (firm's own material); quote for the 4,032 fact strengthened to include the 'Texas datacenter' context. (2) The DCD article (dated 2026-06-05) contains the Dallas sentence, the tens-of-thousands-to-hundreds-of-thousands sentence, and the 100-200MW sentence verbatim; a targeted re-fetch confirmed the previously weak 'self-financed' element via the subtitle 'Company is looking to build and finance its own data center' and confirmed 'Jane Street declined to comment' — reported grade upheld; as_of normalized to the verified DCD article date (2026-06-05) since the Bloomberg original was not independently fetched. (3) The ABAB News piece supports 56 racks, 140 kW/rack, and 8,000 km fiber verbatim and explicitly ties them to the Dwarkesh Patel video tour of the Jane Street facility; article date corrected to 2026-05-19 (researcher had 2026-05-21). Kept at reported with the aggregator caveat preserved in the publisher field; these should only be upgraded to confirmed after direct timestamped verification against the video itself, which could not be fetched. (4) CoreWeave's own press release (2026-04-15) contains all three quotes verbatim — confirmed grade upheld (operator's primary material); the unfetchable SEC 8-K is unnecessary given the primary press release. Nulls: all five survive; the CoreWeave null was trimmed to remove an unverified clause attributing a 'no timeline' observation to DCD and the 8-K reference, restating it strictly on the verified press-release content. Changes are corrective (dates, one quote, one value clarification, null trimming), hence promote_with_changes.

results.reasoning: Adversarial re-verification, 2026-07-05. All 7 cited URLs were fetched live and each supports its fact: (1) HRT's official Facebook post carries the exact '>125 kW per cabinet' Blackwell DLC quote; (2-3) Dell's 2026-05-14 PR confirms XE9685L/AMD EPYC/HGX B200/IRSS/Spectrum-X and 'HRT recently opened a data center at Lefdal Mine Data Centers', with no MW/GPU/rack figures; (4) HRT's LinkedIn post confirms fjord seawater cooling and 'powered entirely by renewable energy'; (5) Google Cloud's 2024-07-18 PR confirms HPC/NVIDIA GPU access with no quantities; (6) Lambda's 2026-05-20 blog confirms HGX B200 supply with no quantities; (7) DCD 2026-05-22 corroborates the Lambda deal (reported grade correct); (8) HRT Beat (2025-08-19) confirms 'hundreds of petabytes'. Confidence grades were re-checked and are correctly assigned (firm/vendor/contractor materials = confirmed; DCD = reported). One change forced the promote_with_changes verdict: the energy fact's quote originally read 'cold seawater siphoned from a nearby fjord...' but the fetched post text reads 'cold seawater from a nearby fjord cools dense GPU clusters designed for large-scale AI workloads' — quote corrected to verified wording; I also normalized the Dell status quote to the fetched 'HRT recently opened...' phrasing. Nulls were independently stress-tested: a fresh MW-focused search surfaced only Lefdal campus-level operator figures (80MW current, 200MW potential — the researcher's misattribution warning about 3i/Lefdal figures is validated), and lefdalmine.com names only BNP Paribas, not HRT. All 7 nulls survive. The researcher's exclusions (unfetchable X post, NVIDIA cookie-walled case study, unverified Bloomberg revenue figure) were correct calls and remain excluded.

results.reasoning: Fetched all 7 URLs. (1) LinkedIn post live; quote verbatim; activity ID 7439690776311365632 decodes to 2026-03-17T15:15 UTC, corroborated by NVIDIA's GTC page dating the Financial Services item 'Tuesday, March 17, 10:00 a.m. PT'. Jump's own channel = confirmed. (2) NVIDIA GTC 2026 page: WebFetch's summarizer initially reported no Jump mention, but the raw HTML (fetched via curl, HTTP 200) contains the section 'Jump Trading to Become One of the First Trading Firms to Adopt NVIDIA Rubin Platform' with the quote verbatim and the #jump-trading anchor present; however the page does NOT state the 72-GPU/36-CPU per-rack spec, so I trimmed the value to 'NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72 (Rubin platform)' — the only substantive change. Tense discrepancy (Jump: 'deployed'; NVIDIA: 'will be among the first to adopt') preserved by keeping the status fact conservative. (3-5) jumptrading.com/ai-ml and /technology return 403 to WebFetch but 200 with a browser UA; all three quotes ('thousands of GPUs', '10,000+ Compute Nodes 5M+ Simulations Run Daily', 'Three large, purpose-built research data centers and over 100 co-located compute environments near global exchanges') verified verbatim in page text = confirmed. (6) Greenhouse boards API job 7781600 live: 'HPC Data Center Technician', location Carrollton, TX; liquid-cooling/high-density quote verbatim; correctly graded inferred (job ad). (7) VAST Data blog live, published 2026-04-20, VAST FWD 2026 talk by Jump's own HPC Systems Engineer; '1.25 TB/s at around 0.6 ms' quote verbatim; graded confirmed as contractor/vendor documentation of Jump's deployment sourced from Jump's engineer. Refutation searches for NVL72 deployment size and Carrollton MW/address figures returned nothing Jump-specific, so all five nulls survive (null 3 enriched with the CyrusOne-colo caveat, null 1 with NVIDIA's cloud-provider-only deployment sizing). Verdict: promote_with_changes solely for the gpu_type value trim.

results.reasoning: All 8 URLs fetched and verified. WebFetch is blocked from web.archive.org, so the five Wayback captures were fetched via curl and grep-verified against the exact quoted strings: (1) Nov 2024 R2823 ad contains 'global estate of data centres with multiple megawatts of capacity' and is Soho Place/London-based; (2) Feb 2025 capture of the same ad contains 'This is a hybrid role based in our new Dallas infrastructure hub' plus the global-estate line; (3) Jun 2025 senior HPC ad contains the Design-and-Build-Engineer sentence and is Dallas-based; (4) eFC Oct 2023 contains 'entirely new engineering and infrastructure hub in Dallas' (author Sarah Butcher); (5) eFC Oct 2025 contains the NorthMark insiders sentence verbatim (3 occurrences, author Alex McMurray) and verifies 'president is former G-Research COO Jen Byrne' and 'Dallas, Texas, home to G-Research's marquee US office'. Live gresearch.com article (15 Jul 2024) verified via WebFetch, but the actual transcript includes disfluencies ('We have a, a tremendous amount of, uh, compute...'), so the quote was corrected to the strictly verbatim fragment 'compute CPU and GPU, multiple eight megawatt data halls' — confirmed grade stands (firm's own site). armadaproject.io and the 2020 G-Research Armada blog verified verbatim; the Armada site does not itself name G-Research as originator, so the publisher description was softened (the origination linkage rests on G-Research's own blog). Confidence grades re-checked: job ads stay inferred even on the firm's own domain; press stays reported; firm's own articles/blogs stay confirmed. Nulls 1 and 4 were adversarially re-tested with fresh searches today and hold (the only vendor case study found, WWT's, is anonymized — matching null 6). Verdict is promote_with_changes solely for the transcript-quote correction and the Armada publisher wording; no facts were dropped and no nulls failed.

The source PDF (brookhavenny.gov DocumentCenter View/1327) is behind a Cloudflare challenge and could not be fetched, so this remains search-snippet-level only and is not submitted as a fact.
results.reasoning: Fetched all three source URLs directly on 2026-07-05; every quote was found verbatim (or as an exact substring of the page sentence). (1) Google Cloud blog (pub. 2024-04-09) contains 'Now, the firm can spin up more than 1 million cores concurrently' and 'At this point — in 2017 — Citadel Securities started working with Google Cloud', plus the on-prem custom-hardware history — graded confirmed as operator material with the firm's Research Platform head as attributed contributor (byline corrected: Bekas is an attributed contributor/speaker, not co-author). (2) Traders Magazine (2023-09-06) carries the 'hundreds of thousands of cores' Bekas quote — credible press, reported. (3) rentec.com About page (the firm's self-declared official website) states both the '50,000 computer cores with over 200 gigabits per second' and '40 terabytes a day' figures — firm's own material, confirmed; correctly flagged as firmwide with location unstated. Adversarial re-search on 2026-07-05 for Citadel Securities MW/GPU figures and Renaissance East Setauket facility specs surfaced nothing new (only Switch's unrelated 'Citadel Campus' in Nevada), so all four nulls stand. Only change from the researcher's submission is cosmetic publisher/byline wording; no fact dropped, no confidence downgraded.

final_facts.value: RMB 1bn (十亿) build cost for Fire-Flyer 2
final_facts.quote: 幻方 AI 投入十亿建设「萤火二号」

final_facts.value: compute capacity doubled in 2022 via 800-port switch interconnect architecture rework
final_facts.quote: 突破了一期的物理限制，算力扩容翻倍

final_facts.value: 56.74M GPU-hours across 1.35M jobs in 2022, >96% average occupancy
final_facts.quote: 全年运行任务 135 万个，共计 5674 万 GPU 时

final_facts.value: 10,000 A100s purchased 2021, pre-export-controls (corroborates existing record)
final_facts.quote: High-Flyer made an investment in 10,000 A100 GPUs in 2021 before any export restrictions.

final_facts.value: 1,100 accelerator cards; total investment ~RMB 200M
final_facts.quote: 「萤火一号」总投资近二亿元，搭载 1100 加速卡，于当年正式投用

final_facts.value: 0.4 MW (400 KW), basketball-court-sized machine room built 2019
final_facts.quote: 在2019年建立了超算机房，占地面积相当于一个篮球场，功耗400KW

final_facts.value: ~50,000 Hopper GPUs (est.: ~10k H800 + ~10k H100 + H20 orders), shared High-Flyer/DeepSeek, geographically distributed
final_facts.quote: We believe they have access to around 50,000 Hopper GPUs

final_facts.value: ~$1.6bn total server capex (analyst estimate)
final_facts.quote: the total server CapEx for DeepSeek is ~$1.6B

final_facts.value: on-site hiring began Apr 2026: server maintenance engineers and delivery managers to oversee data centre launches in Ulanqab
final_facts.quote: the first time DeepSeek has publicly advertised on-site roles tied directly to its computing infrastructure

final_facts.value: Ulanqab on-site DC ops/delivery roles pay up to RMB 30k/month
final_facts.quote: DeepSeek聘人去内蒙草原"守机房"，月薪最高3万元

final_facts.value: June 2026: hiring extended to 'IDC design planning engineer' — self-build/self-operate posture, design through delivery and ops
final_facts.quote: 数据中心高级运维工程师、数据中心高级交付经理和IDC设计规划工程师

final_facts.value: DeepSeek first external round closed 2026-06-16: RMB 51bn raised at ~RMB 400bn valuation
final_facts.quote: 募资总额510亿元

final_facts.value: entire ~RMB 50bn round earmarked for compute infrastructure and productization
final_facts.quote: 拿到的约500亿融资将全部投向算力基建与产品化两大方向

final_facts.value: Bei Ming (北冥) supercomputing cluster built from 2019; GPU-based, high-scalability; no size figure ever disclosed
final_facts.quote: 建设"北冥"超算集群，其中包括高性能、高扩展性的 GPU 集群

final_facts.value: 2022 job ad: procurement manager for overseas/global IDC site selection and leasing
final_facts.quote: 九坤投资在2022年的一份招聘信息中，就透露了"海外选址"的计划

final_facts.value: AUM ~RMB 80bn as of Q4 2025 (context for compute spend capacity)
final_facts.quote: 目前基金资产管理规模约800亿人民币（截至2025年四季度）

final_facts.value: 1,500 GPU cards, 30k CPU cores, 1PB RAM, ~5PB disk, 400 PFlops AI compute (end-2022 target, firm's own figures via press interview)
final_facts.quote: 计算集群将拥有1500张GPU卡、3万CPU核……AI算力将达400P Flops

final_facts.value: cluster claimed within top-200 of global TOP500 at end-2021 (company self-claim relayed by press; not verifiable on published TOP500 list)
final_facts.quote: 高性能计算集群已经位列全球超算TOP500前200名

final_nulls: DeepSeek Ulanqab (T3-006): NO public facility-level figure exists as of 2026-07-05 — no MW capacity, no GPU count, no site investment amount, no land/rack figure. Press coverage (SCMP 2026-04-11, Sina 2026-04-15, Huxiu 2026-06-09, 36Kr 2026-07-02) covers only job ads, regional advantages, and firm-level financing; a fresh 2026-07-05 search surfaced no facility specs. 'GW-scale ambition' language in aggregator posts is narrative, not a disclosed spec.

Ubiquant Bei Ming (T3-007): no public GPU count, PFlops, MW, or location for the Bei Ming cluster has ever been disclosed — the LatePost/163.com article (fetched) describes only a 'high-performance, highly scalable GPU cluster' built from 2019, and Ubiquant's own about page (fetched) never names Bei Ming or any compute figure.

Minghong (T3-008): no public update on cluster scale since the Sept 2022 China Securities Journal interview (1,500 GPUs / 400 PFlops end-2022 target); no MW, no location, no post-2022 GPU figure found — re-searched 2026-07-05 with no Minghong-specific compute results.

High-Flyer: no 'Fire-Flyer 3' (萤火三号) exists in the public record — re-searched 2026-07-05; the last own-disclosed expansion on high-flyer.cn/history is the 2022 Fire-Flyer 2 capacity doubling. Post-export-control fleet size exists only as third-party analyst estimates (SemiAnalysis ~50k Hopper GPUs), never confirmed by the firm.

No public statement from High-Flyer/DeepSeek quantifying export-control impact on its clusters; chip sourcing for V4 (Huawei Ascend 950PR per The Information vs Nvidia Blackwell per Reuters) is contested per SCMP 2026-04-11 (fetched), with no firm confirmation.
results.reasoning: Adversarial pass: fetched all 10 distinct URLs; every one is live and every quote was located in the fetched content, including the four verbatim Chinese quotes on high-flyer.cn/history (plus the 800-port switch detail: 多 800 口交换机互联加核心扩展子树), the SemiAnalysis exact sentences (breakdown ~10k H800 + ~10k H100 + H20 orders; 'shared between High-Flyer and DeepSeek and geographically distributed'), SCMP's 'The move marks the first time...' sentence, the Sina headline, both 36Kr claims (financing sentence: 募资总额510亿元，估值将近4000亿元), the Huxiu earmarking sentence, the LatePost/163 Bei Ming sentence (article confirmed to contain zero quantitative specs), the wallstreetcn 超算地图 quotes, the Ubiquant AUM sentence, and both cs.com.cn Minghong quotes (attributed to a Minghong presentation at the 2022 Lujiazui Forum; figures are explicitly an end-2022 forecast — 预计的2022年底 — which the fact's value already flags). Confidence re-grades hold: firm's own site = confirmed (4 facts + Ubiquant AUM); press = reported; job-ad-derived facts stay inferred even when relayed by press. One change: the 56.74M GPU-hours fact was typed 'energy' but GPU-hours is a utilization metric, not energy — reclassified to 'other'. Known discrepancy preserved: Huxiu (2026-06-09, pre-close) had ~RMB 50bn at RMB 350bn valuation vs 36Kr (post-close) RMB 51bn at ~400bn; the round-size fact correctly cites the later 36Kr figures, and the Huxiu fact is limited to the use-of-proceeds claim, which its URL supports. Nulls re-tested with fresh 2026-07-05 searches: no Ulanqab facility specs, no Fire-Flyer 3, no post-2022 Minghong update surfaced; Bei Ming absence and V4 chip contestation confirmed directly in the fetched articles. All 18 facts survive; verdict is promote_with_changes solely for the fact_type reclassification.

final_facts.value: 600+ PB storage capacity; infrastructure running >110,000 simulations daily (page inconsistently also shows 380+ PB stored and 100,000 simulations elsewhere)
final_facts.quote: uses 600+ PB of storage capacity and infrastructure to run over 110,000 simulations daily

final_facts.value: compute claimed equivalent to a world top-5 supercomputer site (firm's own unquantified marketing claim; no FLOPS, core, or GPU figure given)
final_facts.quote: Computing power that would rank in the world's top 5 supercomputer sites

final_facts.value: processing power 'measured in zettaFLOPS' (firm's characterization, likely cumulative ops; no methodology or hardware count disclosed); 300+ PB stored
final_facts.quote: Employ processing power measured in zettaFLOPS

final_facts.value: 27 MWh/month (Jan 2023) reduced to 10 MWh/month (Jan 2024) — production hosts of ONE trading engineering team only, not firmwide; do not map as a site power figure
final_facts.quote: In January 2023, production hosts consumed 27 MWh of electricity. By January 2024, this figure had dropped to 10 MWh.

final_facts.value: ~60 legacy servers decommissioned by one trading team, replaced with denser 28-core-processor servers; 66% absolute power reduction (single-team scope, not firmwide)
final_facts.quote: They gradually decommissioned approximately 60 legacy servers and reduced their absolute power consumption by 66%.

final_facts.value: ML research GPUs consumed on-demand via Google Cloud GKE Dynamic Workload Scheduler (+80% GPU obtainability); no GPU counts disclosed — extends existing Google Cloud usage evidence on T3-013
final_facts.publisher: Google Cloud (quoting Two Sigma engineer Alex Hays)
final_facts.quote: Dynamic Workload Scheduler improved on-demand GPU obtainability by 80%, accelerating experiment iteration for our researchers.

final_facts.value: live trading production hosts implied to sit on the eastern New Jersey (NYSE) and Chicago (CME) exchange grid areas — firm's own emissions analysis chose these two grids; no facility or colo partner named; supports only an inferred link to existing Tier-1 pins, not a new pin
final_facts.quote: for the grid networks that support the New York Stock Exchange and Chicago Mercantile Exchange

final_nulls: No public GPU count or GPU fleet size exists for Two Sigma, on-prem or cloud — the Apr 2024 Google Cloud blog confirms GPU usage on GKE but gives no numbers. (Caution: search summaries misattribute XTX Markets' 25,000-GPU / 650 PB cluster to Two Sigma; the source article assigns those figures to XTX, with none for Two Sigma.)
No public MW or site-level power figure exists for any Two Sigma facility; the only public energy figures are the single-team 27-to-10 MWh/month case study (Oct 2024 article).
No newer total node/core count has been published since the 2016 CMU ATLAS traces (1,313 nodes / 31,512 cores); the firm now uses only unquantified 'top-5 supercomputer' and 'zettaFLOPS' language.
No data center location or colocation partner is named anywhere in the fetchable public record; targeted searches for Two Sigma + New Jersey colocation/megawatts returned nothing usable.
No Two Sigma engineering-blog or conference talk disclosing cluster size post-2016 was found (Cook scheduler posts and RLlib/Ray material discuss software, not fleet scale).
results.reasoning: All three source URLs were fetched live on 2026-07-05 and every quoted string was found verbatim: the about-us page carries both the '600+ PB / 110,000 simulations' and 'top 5 supercomputer sites' claims (alongside internally inconsistent 380+ PB / 100,000 figures, noted in the fact values); the Oct 2024 sustainability case study carries the zettaFLOPS, 300+ PB, 27-to-10 MWh, ~60-server/66% and NYSE/CME-grid passages, and the fetch explicitly confirmed the MWh figures are one trading engineering team's production hosts, not firmwide; the Google Cloud blog carries the Alex Hays Dynamic Workload Scheduler quote with attribution. Confidence grades hold under strict re-grading: twosigma.com pages are the firm's own materials (confirmed); the Google Cloud blog is the compute operator's own publication quoting the firm's engineer (confirmed); the NYSE/CME grid location signal is the firm's material but the colocation conclusion is triangulated, so it stays inferred. One typing correction: the server-decommissioning fact was re-typed from 'cores' to 'other' since it records a server count and power reduction, not a core total. Adversarial null-testing found one landmine — a web-search summary that attributed '25,000 GPUs and 650 PB' to Two Sigma; fetching the underlying substack article confirmed those figures belong to XTX Markets (Gerko's 'matrices' quote) and the article gives no infrastructure numbers for Two Sigma, so the GPU null survives with a warning appended. Fresh searches for Two Sigma MW figures, named facilities, or colo partners returned nothing, so all five nulls stand. Verdict is promote_with_changes for the minor fact_type fix and clarifying caveats added to values; no facts were dropped.
